name: Get Shucked
note: On-farm oyster bar and Australia's first oyster drive-through at Great Bay, serving oysters harvested and shucked on site daily with Tasmanian wine and beer.

name: Freycinet Marine Farm
note: Farm-gate seafood stall and deck on the road into Freycinet where you buy just-harvested oysters and mussels, plus daily wading farm tours to pick your own.

name: Oyster Bay Tours (Freycinet Marine Farm Taste Experience)
note: Guided wading tour of Freycinet Marine Farm where guests harvest oysters straight off the rack, watch a shucking demo and taste oysters and mussels with local Riesling.

name: Barilla Bay Oysters
note: Working oyster lease near Hobart Airport with a restaurant, retail seafood shop and farm tours covering oyster growing and processing.

name: Melshell Oyster Shack
note: Third-generation family oyster shack on Great Oyster Bay serving golden-shell Angasi and Pacific oysters in casual shuck-and-chat sessions.

name: Tarkine Fresh Oysters
note: North-west Tasmania farm offering tours of its onshore working oyster operation with a shucking demonstration and tastings of its premium oysters.

name: Tasmanian Wild Seafood Adventures
note: Full-day Bruny Island seafood adventure where guests harvest oysters and other wild seafood and dine on the day's catch.

name: Oyster Farm Tours (Coffin Bay)
note: Award-winning wading tour where you put on waders, walk to a semi-submerged deck on a working lease and eat freshly shucked Coffin Bay oysters with wine in the water.

name: Experience Coffin Bay Oyster Farm Tours
note: Boat-based farm-to-fork tours cruising to a remote oyster lease where guests don waders, wade among the beds and eat freshly shucked oysters with local drinks.

name: Pure Coffin Bay Oysters
note: Oyster farmer-led boat tour along Kellidie Bay visiting nursery and original leases with a wading experience and freshly shucked oysters included.

name: 1802 Oyster Bar
note: Waterfront oyster bar and bistro on the Coffin Bay esplanade with a deck overlooking the oyster leases, serving local oysters prepared many ways.

name: SA Premium Oysters (Oyster Deck Experience)
note: Barge trip to a purpose-built floating deck above a working family lease at Smoky Bay where guests shuck fresh oysters and enjoy tastings with wine.

name: Smoky Bay Oysters
note: Family-owned Smoky Bay grower in the Ceduna oyster region offering an authentic working-lease oyster farm experience.

name: Cape Bauer Oysters
note: Streaky Bay grower at 45 Stanley Williams Drive welcoming visitors daily to buy oysters direct, with an A-frame sign showing when the farmers are available.

name: EP Cruises (Ocean to Plate Taster)
note: Half-day Streaky Bay cruise visiting an operational oyster farm where guests harvest and shuck their own oysters and eat them fresh with wine.

name: Turners Oysters & Seafood
note: Family-operated Cowell farm on Oyster Drive selling oysters fresh from the sea and offering tours of its land-based farming and grading facility.

name: Sydney Oyster Farm Tours
note: Hawkesbury River cruise to a working Broken Bay oyster farm where guests learn to shuck and eat a dozen fresh oysters, including an in-water dining experience.

name: Broken Bay Pearl Farm
note: Working pearl and oyster farm on the Hawkesbury offering a farm tour with an Akoya oyster tasting on the water.

name: Holbert's Oyster Farm
note: Five-generation Port Stephens farm outlet on Cromarty Bay where you watch shuckers at work through windows and eat fresh oysters alfresco over the bay.

name: Wallis Lake Fishermen's Co-op
note: Co-op shop on the edge of Wallis Lake selling locally farmed Sydney rock oysters and other fresh seafood direct to the public daily.

name: Armstrong Oysters
note: One of Australia's largest Sydney rock oyster farms, with a shopfront at Short St, Laurieton selling fresh oysters direct to visitors.

name: Rockin' Oysters
note: Camden Haven micro-farm offering pre-booked waterside seafood BBQs and an optional boat tour of the leases at Gogleys Lagoon with oyster sampling.

name: Jim Wild's Oysters
note: Family farm on the Crookhaven River with farm-gate sales of freshly shucked Sydney rock and Pacific oysters, plus guided tours by a world-champion shucker.

name: The Oyster Shed on Wray Street
note: Family farm-gate oyster shed on the Clyde River selling shucked, dressed and whole rock oysters with seating and river views over four generations of farming.

name: Pearly Oyster Bar and Farm
note: Farm-to-plate oyster bar on the Clyde River serving oysters grown on the family's own Batemans Bay leases.

name: Region X Clyde River Oyster Tasting Kayak Tour
note: Guided kayak tour on the Clyde River paddling past oyster racks to meet farmers and sample freshly shucked oysters straight from the water.

name: Oyster Pro
note: Wagonga Inlet farm gate at Narooma where you grab freshly grown rock oysters opened or whole direct from the grower.

name: The Oyster Farmer's Daughter
note: Waterside oyster bar of the Coxon family farm on Wagonga Inlet serving freshly shucked Sydney rock oysters with cocktails, beer and wine.

name: Wheelers Oysters
note: Merimbula-area seafood restaurant and oyster bar with retail sales and a Saturday oyster farm tour tasting oysters straight from Merimbula Lake.

name: The Oyster Barn
note: Casual lakeside oyster bar on western Merimbula Lake serving natural and dressed oysters, including the rare Angasi, with scallops, prawns and BYO wine.

name: Broadwater Oysters
note: Award-winning Pambula Lake farm with lakeside oyster sales, a 'Shuck School' and 'Meet the Farmer' tours of its sustainable Sydney rock oyster operation.

name: Captain Sponge's Magical Oyster Tours
note: Two-hour Pambula Lake punt cruise eating oysters straight from the water while learning oyster husbandry and spotting wildlife.

name: Tathra Oysters
note: Multi-award-winning Nelson Lagoon grower whose 'shellar door' shopfront in Tathra sells fresh oysters to the public from December to June.

name: Brian Orr Oysters (at Mimosa Meats)
note: Wapengo Lake rock oysters sold at the family's Mimosa Meats shopfront in Tathra alongside local produce and seasonal seafood.

name: Wonboyn Rock Oysters
note: Farm gate on Wonboyn Lake near Eden where you can buy award-winning Sydney rock oysters, from cocktail to plate size, and eat them by the lake.

name: Navigate Expeditions
note: Kayak expeditions on the Pambula River estuary paddling among oyster leases with a shucking lesson and tasting.

name: Narooma Bridge Seafoods
note: Wagonga Inlet seafood outlet selling shucked and whole local oysters plus lobster and other seafood direct to the public.

name: Brisbane Oyster Farm Tours
note: Moreton Bay boat tour where guests wade to a lease, shuck 18 fresh oysters and dine at white-clothed tables set up in the water.

name: Straddie Oyster Tours
note: Catamaran tour from Raby Bay to a Moreton Bay oyster farm for a hands-on experience with fresh tastings, lunch and a shucking attempt.

name: Quandamooka Oyster Farm Tours
note: First Nations-led tour walking out at low tide to working leases to learn how oysters are grown, handled and harvested.

name: Kooringal Oysters
note: Sydney rock oyster farm in Moreton Island Marine Park offering champagne-and-oyster tours of the family operation with ocean-fresh oysters.

name: Portarlington Mussel Tours
note: Port Phillip Bay seafood cruise on a restored Huon pine vessel featuring local Angasi oyster and mussel tastings with cooking demonstrations.

name: Untamed Escapes Oyster Farm Tour
note: Albany oyster farm experience where visitors sample Albany rock oysters grown in the Oyster Harbour estuary.

name: Leeuwin Coast (Albany Rock Oysters)
note: Albany-based rock oyster grower in the Oyster Harbour estuary producing Albany rock oysters with a distinctive local flavour profile.

name: Cygnet Bay Pearl Farm
note: Australia's oldest working pearl farm on the Dampier Peninsula offering pearl-oyster farm tours and on-site dining.
